rationale: Both reviews are well-grounded with specific file:line evidence, but X's Major finding (default sleep(level=2) plus the new hard NotImplementedError permanently bricks wake_up/generate) directly foreshadows the real post-merge incident (#4905, relaxed by #4912) and explicitly argues the guard is too strict for the default/common caller path — a stronger, more central gap_hit than Y's. X also more directly engages the two actual ground-truth reviewer threads (enum placement/usage, adequacy of the added regression tests), giving it better recall. Y produces solid, well-evidenced secondary findings (stale docs example, dead `_is_sleeping` reference, stale docstring, untested NotImplementedError path in wakeup endpoint) that also touch the gap criterion via the untested-path comment, but these are more peripheral and don't zero in on the core over-strictness issue with the same severity or actionable clarity as X.
